title: Dryopteris laevigata
taxonomy: Dryopteris laevigata is a species within the fern family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes about 250 species of ferns. The species D. laevigata is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. It is a type of evergreen fern with leathery fronds, typically growing in shaded, humid environments. The classification of D. laevigata places it within the phylum Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants characterized by the production of spores.
uses: Dryopteris laevigata, also known as the smooth buckler fern, is primarily found in the tropical regions of the Pacific. However, limited information is available regarding its medicinal uses.
